title: jasminum bahiense var. fluminense
intro: Jasminum bahiense var. fluminense, also known as the Brazilian jasmine or jasmine of the rivers, is a variety of flowering vine native to Brazil, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and woody stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Jasminum bahiense var. fluminense are said to include relief from anxiety and stress, improved sleep quality, and reduced inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be used to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
